title: Error loading MySQL database results in erased configuration
author: JasonN3
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
Don't save the configuration to the MySQL database until it has successfully loaded the configuration
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->

## Current Behavior
<!--- If describing a bug, tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
If there is an error loading, it will erase the ...

iterations.description: Data loss bug: when MySQL database fails to load, the existing connection configuration is erased instead of preserved, causing users to lose their connections.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Data loss bug: when MySQL database fails to load, the existing connection configuration is erased instead of preserved, causing users to lose their connections.
Approach: Wrap the database load in a try/catch that aborts the merge/overwrite step on failure, preserving the existing in-memory configuration rather than clearing it before the error is surfaced.
